name: chrono
title: Chrono
description: Chrono helps you keep track of your work hours in a simple and flexible way. Clock in, clock out, track breaks, and monitor your daily or weekly time balance — perfect for flexible working hours and trust-based schedules.
author: jeffnawroth

preferences.name: targetWeeklyHours
preferences.title: Weekly Target Hours
preferences.description: Your planned working hours per week.

preferences.name: workDaysPerWeek
preferences.title: Work Days per Week
preferences.description: How many days you typically work each week.

commands.name: clock-in
commands.title: Clock in
commands.subtitle: Start working session
commands.description: Starts tracking your work time and records your current session start.

commands.name: clock-out
commands.title: Clock out
commands.subtitle: End working session
commands.description: Stops tracking and calculates your total working time including breaks and daily delta.

commands.name: toggle-pause
commands.title: Toggle Pause
commands.subtitle: Pause / resume work
commands.description: Toggles your break state and tracks total pause duration automatically.

commands.name: today
commands.title: Today
commands.subtitle: Daily time overview
commands.description: Shows your worked hours, break time, and current daily delta compared to your target hours.

commands.name: week
commands.title: Week
commands.subtitle: Weekly summary
commands.description: Displays your weekly working time, daily breakdown, and overall time balance (plus/minus hours).

commands.name: status
commands.title: Status
commands.subtitle: Live work status
commands.description: Shows your current state (working, paused, or off) and live time delta for today.

commands.name: history
commands.title: Adjust Entries
commands.subtitle: Manual time adjustments
commands.description: Add, edit, or delete sessions and pauses to keep your time records accurate.

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
